vehicle.label: Tiltrotor VTOL Hybrid
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing VTOL with 2 tilting rotor nacelles, BVLOS long-range capable

parts.label: Center Fuselage Spine
parts.spec: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

parts.label: Main Wing (2-piece)
parts.spec: Straight wing, 4S NACA 2412, dual 15mm CFRP spar, 900mm half-span, 160mm chord, 3-layer CFRP skin

parts.label: Nacelle Assembly x 2
parts.spec: Tilting nacelle assembly, 0-90deg tilt axis, 6309 bearing set, servo-driven worm gear, motor and ESC pocket

parts.label: Tilt Servo + Linkage x 2
parts.spec: 25kg-cm DS3218 servo per nacelle, titanium pushrod, dual ball joints, tilt range 0-93deg, crash-tolerant

parts.label: V-Tail x 2 (Ruddervator)
parts.spec: +-35deg V-tail ruddervator pair, MG996R servo each, 30deg dihedral, CFRP sandwich panel, hinge piano wire

parts.label: Landing Gear (Tri)
parts.spec: Tricycle landing gear, spring-steel skid arms, 55mm dia wheels, CG at 1/3 chord, 200mm wheelbase

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide-In)
parts.spec: Belly slide-in battery bay, 6S 8000mAh Li-ion pack, Velcro + strap dual retention, CG position 28% MAC

parts.label: Payload Nose Bay
parts.spec: Interchangeable nose payload bay, Day/IR camera gimbal or survey sensor, 80x60x50mm, 300g payload limit

parts.label: Tilt-Axis Harness + Datum Service Bay
parts.spec: Tiltrotor integration_service module with tilt-axis harness_channel, slack_loop, interface_datum, service_hatch, connector keying, and nacelle-to-wing load-path inspection access

risk.desc: Potential misalignment during assembly due to complex interface geometry.
risk.mit: Implement detailed assembly instructions and training for alignment tools.

risk.desc: Cooling channel may not provide sufficient airflow for avionics bay under extreme conditions.
risk.mit: Conduct thermal testing and consider adding auxiliary cooling fans if necessary.

risk.desc: Camera window may be prone to damage during handling or assembly.
risk.mit: Use protective coverings during transport and assembly phases.

risk.desc: Access cutout may interfere with structural integrity if not properly reinforced.
risk.mit: Perform stress analysis on the cutout area and reinforce if needed.

risk.desc: Locking washer may not provide adequate preload if not torqued correctly.
risk.mit: Include torque specifications and quality checks during assembly.

physics_paths.description: Carries wing spar loads into fuselage
Avionics cooling airflow
Thermal management from GPS boom
Ruddervator actuation motion
Landing gear shock absorption

integration_opportunities.description: Optimize avionics_bay for weight reduction while maintaining cooling airflow

manufacturing_strategy.datum: Primary locating faces and axis rings
manufacturing_strategy.support_orientation: Support structures oriented for minimal overhang
manufacturing_strategy.inspection: Visual inspection and CMM for critical dimensions

verification_focus.description: Test structural integrity under simulated loads
Evaluate thermal performance of avionics bay cooling system

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Wing spar carrying nacelle loads into fuselage spine; V-tail/ruddervator and landing gear attach structure

physics_paths.path: Loads from nacelle tilt mechanism are transferred into the wing spar via bearing seats.
Service access for motor and servo replacement is provided through access covers.
Heat generated by motors and electronics is dissipated through heat sinks attached to the wing structure.

integration_opportunities.opportunity: Add additional stiffeners for increased structural integrity.
integration_opportunities.benefit: Improves wing's ability to handle higher loads.

integration_opportunities.opportunity: Incorporate lightweight materials for weight reduction.
integration_opportunities.benefit: Reduces overall aircraft weight, improving performance.

manufacturing_strategy.datum: Primary locating faces and spar sockets
manufacturing_strategy.support_orientation: Support structures oriented vertically for optimal print quality
manufacturing_strategy.inspection: Visual inspection for surface finish and dimensional accuracy

verification_focus.description: Ensure the mass of the wing is within the specified budget.
Verify that loads from the nacelle are properly transferred to the fuselage spine.
verification_focus.expected_result: No deformation under specified load conditions

verification_focus.description: Check that all service access points are unobstructed and easily accessible.
verification_focus.expected_result: All components can be removed/replaced without difficulty

risk.desc: Potential misalignment of servo linkage due to manufacturing tolerances.
risk.mit: Implement stricter manufacturing tolerances and perform post-manufacture alignment checks.

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient clearance for nacelle rotation.
risk.mit: Review and adjust clearance arc dimensions to ensure adequate rotation space.

risk.desc: Possibility of inadequate cooling for the servo during prolonged operation.
risk.mit: Integrate additional thermal management features such as heat sinks or fins.

risk.desc: Risk of improper sealing at the access cover leading to ingress of contaminants.
risk.mit: Use high-quality seals and conduct pressure tests to ensure proper sealing.

risk.desc: Potential difficulty in replacing the servo due to tight integration with the linkage.
risk.mit: Design the system to allow easy disassembly and reassembly of the servo.

integration_opportunities: Add additional strain relief points for future harness upgrades.
Include a secondary service hatch for redundant access.
